concepts.label: Arrays & Hashing
concepts.description: Basic hash map frequency counting, lookup tables, and array transformations.

concepts.label: Two Pointers
concepts.description: Using two index variables to scan linear structures from opposite ends or single directions.

concepts.label: Sliding Window
concepts.description: Maintaining a dynamic subarray boundary to track maximum or minimum subarrays satisfying a constraint.

concepts.label: Linked Lists
concepts.description: Node pointers traversals, insertions, deletions, and structural reorderings.

concepts.label: Fast & Slow Pointers
concepts.description: Using two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles or find list middle elements.

concepts.label: Recursion Basics
concepts.description: Understanding stack execution flow, recursive base cases, and parameter tracking.

concepts.label: Binary Trees
concepts.description: Hierarchical node structure operations, leaf counts, and path logic.

concepts.label: Tree DFS
concepts.description: Pre-order, in-order, and post-order depth-first search traversals of hierarchical structures.

concepts.label: Graphs
concepts.description: Representing vertices and edges using adjacency lists or adjacency matrices.

concepts.label: Graph DFS
concepts.description: Depth-first traversal of node graphs, managing circular cycles via visited sets.

concepts.label: Backtracking
concepts.description: Exploring all combination choices using recursive state append and state undo transitions.

concepts.label: Topological Sort
concepts.description: Linear ordering of vertices such that for every directed edge u -> v, u comes before v.
